calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-25-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for petitioner Paul Carter, Jr., who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Final Distribution.

The Court addressed the interpretation of paragraph Fifth of the decedent's will as modified by a 2008 codicil. Mr. Wrobel argued that the codicil revoked and replaced the original residue clause by limiting the Salvation Army's gift to the decedent's tangible personal property, and that the decedent's later consolidation of her financial accounts reflected an intent for specific beneficiaries, Paul Carter and the ASPCA, to receive those accounts.

Mr. Carter confirmed that the decedent had consolidated her bank accounts into three Wells Fargo accounts, that she maintained separate Fidelity investment accounts and savings bonds with beneficiary designations to ASPCA, and that at death she owned little beyond clothing and personal effects. Mr. Wrobel stated that notice had been provided to the Salvation Army, and that receipts had been obtained for the donated personal property.

Following discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-20-2025 Continued To Nov-24-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Sep-29-2025 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Aug-26-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. No one appeared to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The will dated July 15, 2008 and the codicil dated November 2. 2008, are admitted to probate. Paul G. Carter, Jr. is appointed as the Administrator of the estate with will annexed with full authority. Bond is set at $300,000.00. Order for the same signed in open Court. A status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for September 29, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If either petition is filed two weeks prior, the status hearing will go off calendar. Frank Mahoney III is appointed probate referee.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Nguyen. Not reported.
